title: High-Performance Seismic Isolation Systems for Bridges.
author: Radhika N
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Bridges are critical lifeline structures whose functionality must be maintained after a seismic event. Conventional seismic design often focuses on structural ductility to dissipate energy, which can lead to significant non-structural damage and potential service disruption. In contrast, high-performance seismic isolation systems offer a superior strategy by decoupling the superstructure from the ground motion, effectively reducing seismic forces transmitted to the bridge and controlling its dynamic response. This paper provides a comprehensive review of advanced seismic isolation technologies, including Lead-Rubber Bearings (LRBs), High-Damping Rubber Bearings (HDRBs), and Friction Pendulum Systems (FPS). The fundamental principles of these systems are discussed, along with their performance characteristics, advantages, and limitations. Furthermore, the paper examines key analytical and numerical modeling techniques used for performance evaluation and presents case studies of successful applications worldwide. Finally, it outlines emerging trends and future research directions aimed at enhancing the resilience and reliability of bridge structures in seismically active regions.
